free_text: Excitation functions of deuteron induced reactions on
natOs up to 50 MeV: Experiments and comparison with
theoretical codes
Natural Os target electro-deposited onto Ni backing.
Thin, natural
abundance, Os-targets were prepared by
electro-deposition on 25 micro-m thick, high purity
Ni backings. The thickness of the 20 Os-targets was
derived from separate individual weighing and
individual determination of the deposit area,
supposing a constant Ni backing thickness. The Os
thickness varied between 0.54 and 2.54 micro-m
(1.2-5.9 mg/cm2).The stacking sequence of the target
was Os/Ni-Hf-Cr/Cu-Al which was repeated 20 times.
The target was irradiated with 50 MeV deuteron beam
of 110 nA for 70 minutes. The Al was used as monitor.
The activity was measured by high resolution gamma
ray spectrometer. Decay data and the Q-values of the
were taken from NUDAT2 and from Q-value calculator of
NNDC.
Chapter 4
The initial beam uncertainty was +-0.2 MeV.
The uncertainty of the beam energy throughout
the stack increases to 1.2 MeV at the last foil.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing components.
surface density of target nuclei (8%)
flux of incident charged particles (6%)
detector efficiency  (5%)
statistical and peak fitting (3-8%),
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE-D
and ALICE-D.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
